content:
# Internal: Incorrect Amount Handling

## Card Status
1. Classify the case as incorrect amount only when the customer says a debit-card or electronic-fund-transfer posting was for the wrong amount, not that the transaction itself was unauthorized.
2. For the incorrect amount category, leave the card action as keep_active. Do not close or replace the card solely because the amount is disputed.
3. If the customer instead describes fraud or account access they did not authorize, use the liability categories in "Debit Card Error Notice And Liability" before deciding the card path.

## Customer Liability
The unauthorized-transaction liability rule applies only to unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud. If one of those categories is reported within 2 business days after discovery, the maximum liability is 50. If reported after that period but within the statement window, the maximum liability is 500. After the statement window, the liability treatment is unlimited liability in this benchmark policy.

For an incorrect amount case that is not an unauthorized category, customer liability is 0.

## Provisional Credit Check
Provisional credit may be considered only for unauthorized_transaction, card_present_fraud, card_not_present_fraud, atm_cash_discrepancy, duplicate_charge. Because incorrect amount is not listed in that allowed set, do not promise provisional credit under this procedure. See "Debit Card Provisional Credit Policy" for the allowed-category list and timing expectations.
title: Internal: Incorrect Amount Handling
